task_completion.description: Import the specified asset into OpenToonz
task_completion.visual_evidence: OpenToonz interface shows 'asset.tif' loaded in the Xsheet, Timeline, or Level Strip. Note that the image might look like a large background/sky.

task_completion.description: Extend the asset exposure to 24 frames
task_completion.visual_evidence: The asset's cell is extended or exposed across 24 frames in the OpenToonz Xsheet or Timeline.

task_completion.description: Animate the asset moving from left to right
task_completion.visual_evidence: Keyframes are visible at the start and end frames. The viewer's motion path or the Function Editor shows the horizontal (X / East-West) position changing from a lower/left value to a higher/right value.

task_completion.description: Apply Ease In and Ease Out motion interpolation
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Function Editor (or Graph Editor) shows an S-shaped (sigmoid) curve for the horizontal position, or the interpolation settings explicitly show 'Ease In/Ease Out' or 'Speed In/Speed Out' applied to the keyframes.

task_completion.description: Set the render output path and format
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Output Settings dialog is visible, with the 'Save In' path set to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/ease_test/' (or '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/ease_test') and the file format set to 'png'.

task_completion.description: Render the animation as a PNG sequence
task_completion.visual_evidence: A rendering progress bar is visible in OpenToonz, or a file manager/terminal window shows multiple PNG files (at least 20) successfully generated in the '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/ease_test/' directory.

integrity.description: Agent used the OpenToonz GUI to create the animation
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active use of the OpenToonz graphical interface (tools, Xsheet, Function Editor) rather than terminal scripts or text editors modifying project files directly.

integrity.description: Output files were generated by the application's render engine
integrity.visual_evidence: The PNG files are generated via OpenToonz's render function (e.g., render progress window is shown), not by running a terminal command (like 'touch' or 'cp') to create dummy files.

integrity.description: Interpolation was used rather than manual per-frame keyframing
integrity.visual_evidence: The ease-in/ease-out effect is achieved using interpolation curves/handles between two main keyframes in the Function Editor, not by manually placing individual keyframes on every single frame.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Asset path: '/home/ga/Desktop/asset.tif' (Note: due to a fallback, the asset might visually appear as a large background image like a sky, rather than a typical character). Output directory: '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/ease_test'. Minimum rendered frames required for success: 20 (though the task asks for 24). Expected motion: Sigmoid velocity curve (slow-fast-slow) where horizontal position is < 20% at the 25% time mark, and > 80% at the 75% time mark.
